stance: Risk-on, momentum-led; rotate within the book toward names with rising analyst revisions and widen breadth, while dialing back unscored single-issuer and falling-revision exposure.
experience_note: Pre-registered watch items, judged next round against NIFTY 500. (1) Does the analyst-revision tilt earn its keep — do the high-ARM adds (LICI, MUTHOOTFIN, EQUITASBNK, GUJGASLTD, COFORGE, SOLARINDS, GLAND) collectively beat the bench and beat the low-ARM names I exited (BPCL, HINDPETRO, ASHOKLEY, BRITANNIA, MFSL)? If the exits outperform my adds, the ARM tilt added no value this round. (2) Conviction-IC: did I size winners bigger than losers? I will check whether my heavier weights (Vedanta parent, GRASIM, MCX, MUTHOOTFIN, LICI) ranked above my lighter ones in realized active return. (3) Beta discipline: did trimming the deep-cyclical/OMC tail actually lower realized beta toward ~1.0 without giving up alpha? If beta stays >1.1, my excess is still mostly leverage, not skill. (4) Was cutting the unscored Vedanta shells right — did they lag the names I redeployed into? I would change my mind on the ARM-led rotation if the rotated-out PSU banks and OMCs outperform the rotated-in NBFCs/gas/healthcare over the next 1-3 months, which would mean revisions were already priced or mean-reverting.
